source: Large-Scale Matrix Factorization with Distributed Stochastic Gradient Descent

concepts.finding: Distributed SGD for large-scale MF. Strategies: (1) data parallelism (partition users/items across workers), (2) model parallelism (partition factors across workers), (3) asynchronous updates with staleness tolerance. Hybrid approach: data-parallel for dense factors, model-parallel for sparse. Handles billions of ratings.
concepts.implication: Distributed keyword embedding training at scale. For millions of keywords with billions of co-occurrences, distributed SGD with hybrid parallelism is necessary. Asynchronous updates with staleness tolerance work well.
concepts.open_question: At what keyword count does distributed training become necessary for keyword embedding? What parallelism strategy is optimal?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Distributed SGD for keyword embedding achieves near-linear speedup up to 32 workers for graphs with 10M+ keyword nodes.
